items.name: accordion
items.description: A disclosure group: several triggers each expand/collapse their own region, single or multiple open. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the open set lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-accordion> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG accordion (per-item button[aria-expanded][aria-controls] + region[aria-labelledby]).

files.content:
@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import io.lievit.wire.AccordionComponent @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui accordion WIRE template -- a disclosure GROUP whose open set lives server-side in AccordionComponent (ADR-0012, Wave 2). Pairs with registry/wire/accordion/AccordionComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open set is server state (a @Wire list); each trigger ARMS its item via the `$set('toggleId', '')` magic (the canonical wire:click="$set(...)" idiom this repo already uses in listing-list.jte), the server re-renders this template, the client morphs it. There is no (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter); the panel BODIES are NOT a JTE Content slot supplied at call time -- they are OWNED markup inside each region. The adopter edits the body region per item directly (this is copy-in source). That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Accordion pattern, Radix Accordion as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - each trigger is a real
<%-- Owned body region for this item: replace with your panel markup (server-rendered). --%>

Panel content for ${label}.

@endfor

items.docs: Register AccordionComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `itemIds` + `labels` (index-aligned) and `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.accordion(...)`. Each trigger arms its item with `l:click="$set('toggleId','<id>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the open set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side). The panel BODY per item is OWNED markup inside the `data-accordion-region` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/accordion")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.description: An inline message banner; severity drives both colour and the ARIA live role (destructive/warning are assertive role=alert, info/success are polite role=status). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

items.name: alert-dialog
items.description: A confirm modal: an interruptive prompt requiring a response (delete this? discard?). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 3), the role=alertdialog specialization of the dialog wire: open-state + title/description/labels + the destructive flag live in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the destructive action + cancel are real l:click buttons, not slots. Renders the dialog wire's overlay/panel structure directly (no nested live instance, no <lv-dialog> island). WAI-ARIA APG alertdialog (role=alertdialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); the backdrop does NOT dismiss (only the explicit buttons do); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

files.content: @import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert-dialog WIRE template -- a confirm modal whose open-state lives server-side in AlertDialogComponent (ADR-0012, Wave 3). Pairs with registry/wire/alert-dialog/AlertDialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the destructive action + cancel buttons fire `confirm` / `cancel` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No , no : this renders the dialog wire's STRUCTURE (overlay + panel + hidden-when-closed) directly, specialized to a confirm prompt. A wire template is rendered with ONLY its own @Wire fields + _component (JteTemplateAdapter, the Wave 0 finding), so one wire component cannot nest another as a live instance; "composing the dialog" therefore means rendering its overlay/panel structure here, owned and server-rendered, which is what this template does. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Alert Dialog pattern, Radix AlertDialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit-island-backed partial cited): - the panel is role="alertdialog" + aria-modal="true" (NOT role="dialog": an alertdialog is an interruptive prompt requiring a response, so the backdrop does NOT dismiss it; the only exits are the explicit confirm / cancel buttons); - aria-labelledby -> the title id; aria-describedby -> the description id when set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree; - the footer is two real

items.docs: Register AlertDialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.alert-dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` from a trigger to show the prompt, `confirm` (the destructive/primary action) or `cancel` to close. Do the real destructive work server-side by overriding `confirm()` (with authz) before it closes, or read the `confirmed` wire flag on the host after the round-trip. An alertdialog is interruptive: the backdrop does NOT dismiss it, only the explicit buttons do. Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-alert-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the `cancel` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=cancel button, which is also the initial-focus element via data-lv-autofocus -- the focus-on-cancel default for an interruptive prompt), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/alert-dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: badge
items.description: A small status pill mapping each variant to a token fill + foreground pair: the shadcn taxonomy (default, secondary, destructive, outline) alongside the status family (neutral, success, warning, danger, info). Pass href for an asChild <a> link (else an inert <span>). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 4): variant-to-colour is pure, so there is no client state and no island. Replaces the <lv-badge> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the badge partial to your JTE root at lievit/badge.jte, reached as @template.lievit.badge(variant=..., label="..."). Variant drives the fill + foreground (+ border for outline) tokens across the shadcn names (default/secondary/destructive/outline) and the status names (neutral/success/warning/danger/info); pass label for plain text or a content slot for rich children (content wins); pass href to render the pill as a real <a> link (asChild). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: breadcrumb
items.description: An accessible breadcrumb navigation trail: <nav aria-label> landmark wrapping an ordered list, real <a href> for non-current items, aria-current='page' on the current (last) item, separator aria-hidden (WAI-ARIA breadcrumb pattern). Pass maxItems to collapse the middle of a long trail to a BreadcrumbEllipsis (root + tail kept), server-pure, no JS. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 4): the trail is the server-rendered route, so there is no client state and no island. Replaces the <lv-breadcrumb> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the breadcrumb partial to your JTE root at lievit/breadcrumb.jte, reached as @template.lievit.breadcrumb(items=...). Pass items as a List<Map<String,String>> root-to-current, each map keyed label (+ optional href); the last item is rendered as the current page (aria-current='page', not a link). Set separator to change the divider glyph (default '/'). Set maxItems > 0 to collapse a long trail's middle to an ellipsis (root + last maxItems-1 kept). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: button
items.description: A token-styled button (or link styled as a button) with Filament-grade height-based sizing (sm/md/lg, shadcn h-9 baseline) that aligns to the form controls in a toolbar, an iconOnly square mode for icon actions, and primary/secondary/destructive/ghost/outline variants with shadcn hover + aria-invalid ring states. The click is wired by the consuming wire template via l:click; the partial renders only the styled control. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the button partial to your JTE root at lievit/button.jte, reached as @template.lievit.button(variant=..., size=..., content=@`...`). size is height-based (sm=h-8/32px, md=h-9/36px the shadcn baseline, lg=h-10/40px) so a button sits flush next to a form control in a toolbar; set iconOnly=true (with ariaLabel) for a square icon-only action button. Wire the action with l:click on the rendered element from the consuming wire template, or pass href to render a real link. Extra attributes go through two channels by trust: attrs is a TRUSTED raw string ($unsafe, static author-typed strings ONLY -- wire directives, target/rel) and dataAttrs is a SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes whose values are HTML-escaped (the only safe way to carry user/DB-derived data; keys are the data-attribute name without the data- prefix, simple identifiers). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: button-group
items.description: Joins buttons (and/or nested groups, selects, input-groups) into a segmented control: a role=group flex box whose CSS collapses the shared inner rounding + borders between adjacent children (shadcn's ButtonGroup, horizontal or vertical). Pure layout over the children the caller passes in, no client state; focus-visible children raise their z-index so the ring is never clipped by a neighbour's border. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui button-group partial -- groups buttons into a segmented control with shared borders + collapsed inner rounding (the "joined buttons" look). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The group is pure layout/styling over the buttons the adopter passes in; no client state. (A toggle-group, which DOES carry pressed-state, is the interactive Lit island -- a separate component.) STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's ButtonGroup is a `role="group"` div whose CSS collapses the rounding + shared border between adjacent children ([&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-l-none + border-l-0, vertical flips axis). We map that structure verbatim to Tailwind utilities so ANY child (button, our input-group, a select) joins seamlessly -- shadcn wins on styling/markup ergonomics here and there is no a11y behaviour to defer to Radix beyond the group role. A11y (Radix/shadcn model): role="group" + an optional aria-label so AT announces the set as one labelled group. Keyboard behaviour stays each button's own (Tab moves between them); a button-group is not a radio/toolbar, so no roving tabindex is imposed. focus-visible buttons raise their z-index so the focus ring is never clipped by a neighbour's border. Params: gg.jte.Content content the buttons (and/or nested groups/inputs) to join. String orientation = "horizontal" horizontal | vertical. String label = null aria-label for the group; null => unlabelled group. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root. Usage: @template.lievit.button-group(label = "Text alignment", content = @` @template.lievit.button(content = @`Left`) @template.lievit.button(content = @`Center`) @template.lievit.button(content = @`Right`) `) @template.lievit.button-group(orientation = "vertical", content = @` `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String orientation = "horizontal" @param String label = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var horizontal = !orientation.equals("vertical");} !{var axis = horizontal ? "flex-row" : "flex-col";} !{var joinReset = horizontal ? "[&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-l-none [&>*:not(:first-child)]:border-l-0 [&>*:not(:last-child)]:rounded-r-none" : "[&>*:not(:first-child)]:rounded-t-none [&>*:not(:first-child)]:border-t-0 [&>*:not(:last-child)]:rounded-b-none";}
${content}

Pass the buttons (or nested groups / selects / input-groups) as the content slot; they join seamlessly with collapsed inner rounding and shared borders. Set orientation="vertical" to stack on the vertical axis, and label for the group's aria-label (announced as one labelled group). Keyboard behaviour stays each child's own (Tab moves between them); a button-group is not a radio/toolbar, so no roving tabindex is imposed. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: calendar
items.description: A full event calendar (month/week/day) rendered server-first (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). The grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor date and the filter all live in typed Java; the server renders the grid as HTML and the client morphs each re-render. The wire optimization toolkit absorbs round-trip latency: l:init/skeleton for first paint, l:loading feedback, l:model.debounce filter, l:click navigation. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag onto a day cell -- is a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer that fires the moveEvent action on drop (no Lit, no @event-calendar). Retires the <lv-calendar> Lit island and gest's ht-calendar escape-hatch. WAI-ARIA APG grid pattern.

files.content:
@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import io.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent @import io.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent.Cell @import io.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent.Event @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui calendar WIRE template -- a full event calendar (month/week/day) whose grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor and the filter all live SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). Pairs with registry/wire/calendar/CalendarComponent.java + the calendar.ts drag enhancer. This is the single heaviest piece of the refactor: it retires both the Lit island AND gest's ht-calendar (@event-calendar) escape-hatch. There is NO Lit, NO @event-calendar, NO . TIER: WIRE. The grid is server-rendered HTML from the typed model (CalendarComponent.weeks()); the view switch + prev/next/today are l:click actions (the server re-renders the grid, the client morphs it); the filter is a @Wire string bound l:model.debounce.250ms so there is NO round-trip per keystroke. The wire OPTIMIZATION toolkit absorbs the round-trip latency a server-rendered drag-grid would otherwise pay: - Initial grid: when not yet loaded the template ships a SKELETON whose l:init="load" fires the `load` action right after bind (the wire:init parity), so first paint is immediate and the real grid arrives on the next morph; l:loading regions give instant feedback while a call resolves. - Filter/typeahead: l:model.debounce.250ms -> server re-filters a few hundred ms after the last key. - Navigation: l:click prev/next/today/viewMonth/viewWeek/viewDay re-render the resolved period. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag-resize/drag-create -- is the calendar.ts enhancer: on drop it $set's the dragged event id + the target ISO date into the wire and $call's `moveEvent`; the server owns the event store, moves the event in Java and re-renders. addEventListener only, CSP-clean. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Grid pattern for the month layout (the same model the dropped island cited): the grid is a , weekday headers are
, each day is a carrying aria-selected; today carries aria-current="date". A server-rendered grid + a tiny drop enhancer beats a client grid: the events and the layout are both server-held, so nothing can fail to project (the slot bug) and the layout cannot drift; the only client code is the irreducible drop gesture. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Grid): table[role=grid][aria-label], th[role=columnheader], td[role=gridcell] [aria-selected], the today cell aria-current="date"; the toolbar buttons carry aria-labels; the filter input is a labelled searchbox; the loading region is aria-busy while a call is in flight. Params: view String the visible view ("month" | "week" | "day"; round-trips) anchor String the ISO date the visible range is built around (round-trips) filter String the live filter query (bound by l:model.debounce.250ms) selected String the clicked day ISO (server-owned; set via $set) loaded boolean false on mount (skeleton + l:init), true once `load` has fired dragEventId String the event id the drag enhancer arms before moveEvent (round-trips) dragToDate String the ISO day the dragged event is dropped onto (round-trips) _instance CalendarComponent the live instance (reads the server-built grid + the period label) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, seed allEvents in the component from real data keyed by the visible range, register the component as a PROTOTYPE @Bean, call enhanceCalendars(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() so a day-cell drop moves the event server-side): @template.lievit.calendar(...) -- mounted by the wire pipeline. --%> @param String view @param String anchor @param String filter @param String selected @param boolean loaded @param String dragEventId @param String dragToDate @param CalendarComponent _instance @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var label = _instance.periodLabel();} !{List headers = _instance.weekdayHeaders();} !{List> weeks = _instance.weeks();}
<%-- Toolbar: period label + nav + view switch + the debounced filter. --%>
${label} <%-- l:loading region: instant feedback while any wire call for this calendar is in flight. --%> Loading…
@if(!loaded) <%-- First-paint skeleton: l:init fires `load` right after bind so the real grid arrives on the next morph; the skeleton is what the user sees instantly (initial-latency toolkit). --%>
@for(int i = 0; i < 35; i++)
@endfor
@else @for(String h : headers) @endfor @for(List week : weeks) @for(Cell cell : week) @endfor @endfor
${h}
${cell.dayOfMonth()}
@for(Event ev : cell.events()) ${ev.title()} @endfor
@endif <%-- The drag enhancer arms these over the wire ($set) before $call('moveEvent'); kept as hidden mirrors so a hand-built POST (JS off) can still move an event, and so the fields are visibly part of the owned template, not magic. --%>

items.docs: Register CalendarComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `allEvents` from your repository keyed by the visible range (rangeStart()..rangeEnd()) in the constructor / a @LievitRender hook -- the seed list is example data. Render it via the wire mount / @template.lievit.calendar(...); the cells + events are OWNED template markup (NOT a slot). Call enhanceCalendars(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() so dragging an event chip onto a day cell moves it server-side; add server-side authz in moveEvent(). The l:init skeleton makes first paint immediate; the l:model.debounce filter and l:click navigation re-render server-side. The @LievitComponent(template="lievit/calendar") name + the io.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.

items.name: card
items.description: A surface container with the full shadcn slot set: title / description / action compose the header, content is the body, footer the optional bottom region (each a Content slot, the server-first equivalent of CardTitle/CardDescription/CardAction/CardContent/CardFooter). A title makes it a labelled region (role=region + aria-labelledby). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui card partial -- a surface container with the full shadcn slot set. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island generated a header id at runtime only to wire aria-labelledby; the server can mint that id deterministically from the caller's `headingId`, so no client is needed. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's Card is a composition of distinct slots -- Card / CardHeader / CardTitle / CardDescription / CardAction / CardContent / CardFooter. In JTE that maps to ONE partial with a Content slot per region (the server-first equivalent of the named sub-components): `title` + `description` + `action` compose the header, `content` is the body, `footer` is the optional bottom region. The header region renders when any of title / description / action is supplied; the footer when `footer` is supplied. The elevation reads --lv-shadow-sm; border + radius read tokens too, so a theme retints the whole surface. TOKEN FIDELITY (shadcn): the surface reads --lv-color-card / --lv-color-card-fg (NOT the page bg/fg) so a card tints distinctly from the page; radius is --lv-radius-xl (shadcn rounded-xl). shadcn's card is BORDERLESS between sections: one flex column with a uniform gap + vertical padding (the level-6 spacing token) and the same horizontal padding per region, no ruled header/footer. Back-compat: the original `heading` String still renders a plain text title; when both `heading` and a rich `title` slot are given, the slot wins (content-over-scalar precedence, the same rule badge.jte / kbd.jte use). A11y (WAI-ARIA region): a title (slot or heading) present => role="region" + aria-labelledby pointing at the title id, so assistive tech announces the section by its title; no title => no landmark (a bare surface). The CardAction sits in the header beside the title (shadcn pins it to the header's end), so the section's primary control is announced within the labelled region. Params: gg.jte.Content title = null rich card title; wins over `heading` when both are set. String heading = null plain-text title (back-compat); renders a header when set. String headingId = "lv-card-heading" id linking aria-labelledby to the title. gg.jte.Content description = null a muted subtitle under the title (CardDescription). gg.jte.Content action = null a control pinned to the header end (CardAction). gg.jte.Content content the card body (CardContent). gg.jte.Content footer = null an optional bottom region (CardFooter). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the card. Usage: @@template.lievit.card(content = @@`

Plain surface.

`) @@template.lievit.card(heading = "Listings", headingId = "listings-card", content = @@`

...

`) @@template.lievit.card( title = @@`Team plan`, description = @@`Billed monthly`, action = @@``, content = @@`

2 of 5 seats used.

`, footer = @@`Renews 1 Jul`) --%> @param gg.jte.Content title = null @param String heading = null @param String headingId = "lv-card-heading" @param gg.jte.Content description = null @param gg.jte.Content action = null @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content footer = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var hasTitle = title != null || (heading != null && !heading.isBlank());} !{var hasHeader = hasTitle || description != null || action != null;}
@if(hasHeader)
@if(hasTitle)
@if(title != null)${title}@else${heading}@endif
@endif @if(description != null)
${description}
@endif
@if(action != null)
${action}
@endif
@endif
${content}
@if(footer != null)
${footer}
@endif

items.name: chart
items.description: A server-rendered static bar chart as inline SVG: one <rect> per typed @param data point, coloured by the --lv-color-chart-N tokens, with axis labels and a WAI-ARIA img role (aria-label + in-SVG <title> text alternative, per-bar aria-label). Zero JS, no canvas, no client charting lib, CSP-clean. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-chart> Lit island. An interactive chart (hover tooltip, zoom) is an escape-hatch typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG, not shipped.

files.content:
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui chart partial -- a SERVER-rendered static bar chart as inline SVG (ADR-0012).
  The bars, axis labels and accessible title are all emitted from typed @param data; there is
  NO JavaScript, NO <canvas>, NO client charting library. Replaces the dropped <lv-chart> Lit
  island, whose hover tooltip + lv-point event were the only client behaviour and are not shipped.

  TIER: PARTIAL (server-rendered SVG). A chart is a pure projection of a fixed dataset: given the
  values, the picture is fully determined server-side, so a client renderer added only the risk of
  the silent-projection bug (the exact class that motivated the pivot) for no decision the server
  could not make. The values arrive via @param from the controller's typed model and the partial
  emits declarative <svg> markup the browser draws with zero boot. INTERACTIVE charting (hover
  tooltips, zoom, live updates) is an ESCAPE-HATCH, not shipped: an adopter who truly needs it drops
  a typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG in their own frontend/src/ (the documented seam), the
  way gest keeps ht-calendar. lievit-ui ships no charting island.

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the simplest useful shape the island supported is the bar
  chart (its default type="bar"), and a bar is the one chart whose geometry is a trivial server
  computation (a rect per datum, height = value / max). shadcn's chart guidance and the WAI-ARIA
  img role agree: a static data graphic is a single role="img" with a text alternative, NOT a
  widget. So the partial is one <svg role="img"> with an <title>/aria-label for the accessible
  name, one <rect> per data point coloured by the --lv-color-chart-N tokens (the same token cascade
  the island used), a baseline, and per-bar category labels under the plot. Native SVG beats a
  bundled lib on every axis here: a11y (the title is the text alternative a screen reader reads),
  zero dependency, and SSR-native declarative markup.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA img): the <svg> carries role="img" + aria-label (the chart's accessible name) and
  an in-SVG <title> as the first child (the SVG-native text alternative). Each bar also carries an
  aria-label="<category>: <value>" so the individual data is reachable; the decorative axis text is
  aria-hidden so the value is not announced twice.

  Params:
    String   label                accessible name for the whole chart (role=img aria-label + <title>).
    String[] categories           x-axis labels, one per data point.
    int[]    values               bar values, aligned positionally with categories.
    String   color    = "var(--lv-color-chart-1)"  bar fill; defaults to the chart-1 token.
    int      width    = 600       SVG viewBox width.
    int      height   = 300       SVG viewBox height.
    boolean  showAxis = true      render the category labels under the plot.
    String   cssClass = ""        extra utility classes on the <svg>.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.chart(label = "Listings closed", categories = months, values = closed)
    @@template.lievit.chart(label = "Leads", categories = sources, values = leads, color = "var(--lv-color-chart-3)")
--%>
@param String label
@param String[] categories
@param int[] values
@param String color = "var(--lv-color-chart-1)"
@param int width = 600
@param int height = 300
@param boolean showAxis = true
@param String cssClass = ""

!{
  int padTop = 12;
  int padRight = 12;
  int padBottom = showAxis ? 28 : 12;
  int padLeft = 36;
  int plotW = width - padLeft - padRight;
  int plotH = height - padTop - padBottom;
  int n = Math.max(values.length, 1);
  double bandW = (double) plotW / n;
  double barW = bandW * 0.7;
  int max = 1;
  for (int v : values) if (v > max) max = v;
}

<svg
  data-slot="chart"
  role="img"
  aria-label="${label}"
  viewBox="0 0 ${width} ${height}"
  preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet"
  class="block h-auto w-full ${cssClass}"
  style="font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);overflow:visible;"
>
  <title>${label}</title>

  <%-- baseline --%>
  !{int baseY = padTop + plotH;}
  <line x1="${padLeft}" y1="${baseY}" x2="${width - padRight}" y2="${baseY}" stroke="var(--lv-color-border)" stroke-width="1" />

  @for(int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
    !{int v = values[i];}
    !{double bandX = padLeft + bandW * i;}
    !{int x = (int) Math.round(bandX + (bandW - barW) / 2.0);}
    !{int barH = (int) Math.round(plotH * ((double) v / max));}
    !{int y = baseY - barH;}
    !{int bw = (int) Math.round(barW);}
    !{String cat = i < categories.length ? categories[i] : String.valueOf(i);}
    <rect
      data-slot="chart-bar"
      x="${x}" y="${y}" width="${Math.max(bw, 1)}" height="${Math.max(barH, 0)}"
      rx="2"
      fill="${color}"
      aria-label="${cat}: ${v}"
    ></rect>
    @if(showAxis)
      !{int labelX = (int) Math.round(bandX + bandW / 2.0);}
      <text
        x="${labelX}" y="${height - 8}"
        text-anchor="middle"
        aria-hidden="true"
        fill="var(--lv-color-muted)"
        style="font-size:11px;"
      >${cat}</text>
    @endif
  @endfor
</svg>

items.name: data-table
items.description: A server-sorted, server-paginated data table: a real <table> whose sort and page are SERVER facts (sortable headers are real <a href> re-sort links carrying aria-sort, paging via the pagination partial). Rows come in via a Content slot (caller owns the cells). Empty state. Zero JS, zero client sort, CSP-clean. The presentational primitive the kit's admin list composes.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table (static JTE partial - server-sorted/paginated table) The SERVER-FIRST data table: a real whose sort and page are SERVER facts. Sortable column headers are real re-sort links (the server produces the next sort URL per column); clicking one re-sorts on the server and re-renders. Pagination is the windowed pagination partial (real page links). The empty state renders a single spanning row when there are no rows. ZERO client JavaScript, ZERO client sort. TIER (why this is a partial, not a Lit island / wire component): the genuinely STATEFUL server-sorted/paginated table is OWNED by lievit-kit's admin list -- the ListingListComponent wire holds the page/sort/search/filter as @Wire fields, AdminListView.of(...) reads one bounded page, and l:click="toggleSort/prev/next" actions re-render it. This partial is NOT that engine; it is the presentational PRIMITIVE the kit (and any plain Spring controller) composes to RENDER one page. The kit's listing-list.jte hand-rolls this exact
///pagination markup; this partial generalises it so the markup, the aria-sort contract, and the sort-link affordance live in one owned place instead of being copy-pasted per resource. The old Lit island (client sort + client pagination, fully in the browser) is removed by ADR-0012: the table is server-first, the kit holds the state, this partial draws it. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule). shadcn's Data Table is a TanStack-Table client grid (sort / paginate / select all in JS); the WAI-ARIA APG "sortable table" pattern is a plain
whose sorted
carries aria-sort and whose header is an in-cell control that re-sorts. For a server-first kit the APG sortable-table pattern wins on (1) a11y (real table semantics, the sort control is a real link that works with JS off) and (5) robustness (the sort and page survive a reload because they are URL state, not in-memory client state). shadcn's grid is rejected: it puts the sort/page truth in the browser, the exact silent-client-state class the pivot removes. So: a real // (composing the table primitive's look),
headers, aria-sort on the active column, the sort affordance as a real (the kit emits the next-sort URL), and the pagination partial for paging. ROWS via a Content slot (data down, the caller owns the cells). The caller renders one @template.lievit.data-table.row(...) per data item inside `rows`, each holding @template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks (or composes the existing table.row / table.cell primitives). This mirrors the kit's typed Cell switch: the partial owns the chrome + the sort/page contract, the caller owns what a cell shows (a badge, a link, an icon, escaped text). No row data is hardcoded here; it arrives via the slot from the controller's typed model. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG, sortable table): real /// rows (caller owns them; one row per data item). int rowCount = -1 number of data rows; <= 0 renders the empty state. -1 = unknown (trust the slot; never render the empty row). String emptyText = "No data" text of the empty-state row. String caption = null optional
; the active sort column's carries aria-sort="ascending|descending" (others "none"); the sort control is a real so it is keyboard reachable and works with JS off; a visible sort indicator glyph (chevron-up/down for asc/desc, chevrons-up-down for unsorted) is aria-hidden (the aria-sort state is the accessible signal); the optional
is the table's accessible name; the empty state is one row spanning all columns. The pagination partial owns its own nav landmark + aria-current. Params: List headers column header labels, in order (REQUIRED). List sortUrls = null per-column next-sort URL; "" / null = that column is not sortable (no link). Same length / order as `headers`. List sortStates = null per-column active sort: "asc" | "desc" | "" (none). Same length / order as `headers`. null = nothing sorted. Content rows the (accessible name). String ariaLabel = null aria-label when there is no visible caption. int page = 0 current page (1-based); a page < 1 hides the pager. int totalPages = 0 total pages; <= 1 hides the pager. String pageHrefPattern = null printf pattern for a page URL (one %d = the page number); null hides the pager. Forwarded to @template.lievit.pagination. String klass = "" extra classes on the . Usage: @@template.lievit.data-table( headers = view.headers(), sortUrls = sortUrls, // controller builds the next-sort href per column sortStates= sortStates, // "asc"/"desc"/"" per column from the active Sort caption = "Listings", rowCount = view.rows().size(), page = view.pagination().page(), totalPages= view.pagination().totalPages(), pageHrefPattern = "/admin/listings?page=%d", rows = @@` @@for(var row : view.rows()) @@template.lievit.data-table.row(content = @@` @@template.lievit.data-table.cell(content = @@`${row.id()}`) `) @@endfor `) --%> @param java.util.List headers @param java.util.List sortUrls = null @param java.util.List sortStates = null @param gg.jte.Content rows @param int rowCount = -1 @param String emptyText = "No data" @param String caption = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param int page = 0 @param int totalPages = 0 @param String pageHrefPattern = null @param String klass = "" !{var colCount = headers.size();} !{var showPager = pageHrefPattern != null && page >= 1 && totalPages > 1;}
@if(caption != null && !caption.isBlank()) @endif @for(int i = 0; i < colCount; i++) !{var label = headers.get(i);} !{var url = (sortUrls != null && i < sortUrls.size()) ? sortUrls.get(i) : null;} !{var sortable = url != null && !url.isBlank();} !{var state = (sortStates != null && i < sortStates.size()) ? sortStates.get(i) : "";} !{var sorted = state != null && (state.equals("asc") || state.equals("desc"));} !{var ariaSort = !sortable ? null : (state.equals("asc") ? "ascending" : (state.equals("desc") ? "descending" : "none"));} @endfor @if(rowCount == 0) @else ${rows} @endif
${caption}
@if(sortable) ${label} @if(state.equals("asc")) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-up", size = "0.85em") @elseif(state.equals("desc")) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down", size = "0.85em") @else @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevrons-up-down", size = "0.85em") @endif @else ${label} @endif
${emptyText}
@if(showPager)
@template.lievit.pagination(current = page, total = totalPages, hrefPattern = pageHrefPattern)
@endif

@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dialog WIRE template -- an accessible modal dialog whose open state lives server-side in DialogComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/dialog/DialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the body + footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the heading id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Dialog"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-dialog hooks): zero open-state in the client, only the focus mechanics. Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action; the close button does too. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the heading + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) heading String dialog title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty hides the header) description String supporting line; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

items.name: field
items.description: The FormField + FieldError orchestration: a label + control + description/error wrapper that auto-derives the accessible wiring (aria-invalid, the description/error ids the control points its aria-describedby at, the destructive label cue) from the per-field error, the server-first analogue of shadcn's FormControl/FormMessage. Supports vertical/horizontal/responsive orientation and a FieldContent slot. Ships the FieldGroup, FieldSet/FieldLegend and FieldSeparator child partials.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui field partial -- the FormField + FieldError orchestration (label + control + description/error) with the accessible wiring auto-derived from the error, server-side. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout + the accessible label/error wiring; no client state. The control is supplied by the caller as a `control` Content slot (data down). This is the server-first analogue of shadcn's FormField/FormControl/FormMessage: validation runs server-side, the controller passes `error` down, and the partial does the aria wiring the React `useFormField()` hook does (compute the description + message ids, set aria-describedby + aria-invalid), so the adopter stops hand-wiring error ids. AUTO-WIRING (the Form gap this closes): shadcn's FormControl sets `id`, `aria-describedby` (description id, plus the message id when there is an error) and `aria-invalid` on the control. JTE Content slots cannot receive computed params, so the wiring values are DETERMINISTIC from `forId` and the partial both (1) renders the description/message regions with those exact ids and (2) exposes them through the `controlDescribedBy` / `controlInvalid` doc contract below so the caller threads them into its control once. The ids never have to be guessed: description id = "-description" (rendered only when `description` is set) message id = "-error" (rendered only when `error` is set, role=alert) aria-describedby the control must carry = the space-joined present ids aria-invalid the control must carry = (error is set) STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn v4 converged Form* onto the Field primitives, so the Field IS the form orchestration: a vertical (or horizontal/responsive) stack of a Label, the control, an optional muted FieldDescription and a FieldError region carrying role="alert". We reproduce that exactly, token-styled. `orientation` mirrors shadcn's Field orientation (vertical default, horizontal label-beside-control, responsive container-query switch); the optional `content` slot is shadcn's FieldContent (keeps descriptions aligned in horizontal). A11y (shadcn / WAI-ARIA model): the label binds to the control by `forId` and carries `data-error` when invalid (shadcn's destructive-label cue). The description is a muted

with id `-description`; the error is a `role="alert"`

with id `-error` (role=alert already implies an assertive live region, so no extra aria-live). The control must point its `aria-describedby` at those ids and set `aria-invalid` when `error` is set. The required marker on the label is `aria-hidden`. Params: String forId id of the control (binds the label + derives the description/error ids). String label = null visible label text rendered for the control. gg.jte.Content control the form control markup (e.g. an @@template.lievit.input(...) call). gg.jte.Content content = null FieldContent slot: label + control side-by-side helper text (horizontal). String description = null muted helper text (FieldDescription); shown alongside the error. String hint = null deprecated alias for `description` (kept for back-compat). String error = null validation error message; when set, shown in a role=alert region + data-invalid. boolean required = false renders a required marker on the label. String orientation = "vertical" "vertical" | "horizontal" | "responsive" field layout. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.lievit.field( forId = "email", label = "Email", required = true, description = "We never share it.", error = errors.email(), control = @@`@@template.lievit.input(name = "email", id = "email", describedBy = "email-description email-error", invalid = errors.email() != null)` ) @@template.lievit.field(orientation = "horizontal", forId = "remember", label = "Remember me", control = @@`@@template.lievit.switch(name = "remember", id = "remember")`) --%> @param String forId @param String label = null @param gg.jte.Content control @param gg.jte.Content content = null @param String description = null @param String hint = null @param String error = null @param boolean required = false @param String orientation = "vertical" @param String cssClass = "" !{var hasError = error != null && !error.isBlank();} !{var desc = (description != null && !description.isBlank()) ? description : hint;} !{var hasDesc = desc != null && !desc.isBlank();}

@if(label != null && !label.isBlank()) @template.lievit.label(forId = forId, text = label, required = required, error = hasError) @endif @if(content != null)
${content}
@endif ${control} @if(hasDesc)

${desc}

@endif @if(hasError)

${error}

@endif

TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. items.name: file-upload
items.description: A server-rendered drag-and-drop dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path: a native <input type=file l:upload> POSTs the bytes out-of-band, the endpoint returns a signed temp-file reference, and the wire sets the server-held file list (the bytes never ride the snapshot). Remove drops a file from the server-held list. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-file-upload> Lit island. WAI-ARIA dropzone-as-button + labelled file list. The runtime upload directive (l:upload) is ready (runtime/features/uploads.ts).

files.content:
@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import io.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent @import io.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent.UploadedFile @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui file-upload WIRE template -- a server-rendered dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/file-upload/FileUploadComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The dropzone + the selected-file list are server-rendered HTML. The upload itself is the wire runtime's upload directive: the native POSTs the chosen bytes out-of-band to the upload endpoint (runtime features/uploads.ts), which returns a signed temp-file reference; the wire sets the bound `files` field to that reference and re-renders this list. The bytes never ride the snapshot (state-never-code), only the reference does. A remove arms the file name via $set then a confirm action drops it from the server-held list. No . Runtime upload directive: READY (runtime/features/uploads.ts installs l:upload). The adopter wires the SERVER half (the /lievit/upload controller that stores to temp + returns the signed ref); that is application-specific. If you prefer a plain multipart POST instead of the wire upload, swap the l:upload input for inside a
and handle the upload in a controller (the partial recipe). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG dropzone-as-button + a labelled file list (the model the dropped island cited): the zone is role="button" tabindex=0 with aria-describedby -> the hint; the native is visually hidden but reachable; each file row has a labelled remove button. Native input + l:upload beats a custom uploader: the platform owns the file picker and drag-drop, the wire owns the transport, the server owns the list. A11y: div[role=button][aria-label][aria-describedby], visually-hidden input[type=file], ul[aria-label="Selected files"] with per-row aria-label="Remove ".   • ${file.name()} ${FileUploadComponent.formatSize(file.size())} @if(pendingRemove.equals(file.name())) @else @endif
    • @endfor
@endif

items.name: form
items.description: The server-first Form: a native <form> wrapper that owns a form-level validation message region (role=alert) and stacks its field children, the server-rendered analogue of shadcn's Form (FormProvider) + a top-level FormMessage for the error not tied to a single field. Pairs with the field partial, which does the per-field aria wiring. JS-off it POSTs natively; with the wire runtime the same markup submits over the wire.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui form partial -- the server-first Form: a native
wrapper that owns a form-level validation message region and stacks its @@template.lievit.field children. The server-rendered analogue of shadcn's Form (FormProvider) + a top-level FormMessage for the error that is not tied to one field (e.g. "Invalid email or password"). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. No client state: validation runs server-side, the controller passes `error` (form-level) + each field's error down, and this partial does the aria wiring. JS-off the form POSTs natively; with the wire runtime present the same markup submits over the wire. This is why shadcn's Form has no real counterpart here beyond layout-only Field: the missing piece was the server validation->message orchestration, which is this + field.jte. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's Form is react-hook-form's FormProvider (pure JS context). A server-first stack has no client form context to provide: the truth is the POST and the controller's validation result. So the "Form" here is the native + a form-level message slot. Field-level wiring (id/aria-describedby/aria-invalid) is auto-derived in @@template.lievit.field from the per-field error; the form-level error gets its own role=alert region whose id (`-error`) the form references via aria-describedby when present. A11y (WAI-ARIA): a native (the platform form landmark). The form-level error is a `role="alert" aria-live="assertive"` region with id `-error`, referenced by the form's aria-describedby only while present, so a screen reader announces the submit failure. Each field carries its own message via @@template.lievit.field. Params: String id = null the form id (derives the form-level message id `-error`). String action = null the form action URL (native POST target). String method = "post" native method. String error = null form-level validation message (not tied to a single field). gg.jte.Content content the form body (fields, field groups, actions). gg.jte.Content header = null optional header content rendered above the error region. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the . Usage: @@template.lievit.form(id = "login", action = "/login", error = errors.form(), content = @@` @@template.lievit.field(forId = "email", label = "Email", error = errors.email(), control = @@`@@template.lievit.input(name = "email", id = "email", describedBy = "email-error", invalid = errors.email() != null)`) @@template.lievit.button(type = "submit", content = @@`Sign in`) `) --%> @param String id = null @param String action = null @param String method = "post" @param String error = null @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content header = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var hasError = error != null && !error.isBlank();} !{var formId = (id != null && !id.isBlank()) ? id : null;} !{var errorId = formId != null ? formId + "-error" : "form-error";} @if(header != null)${header}@endif @if(hasError)
${error}
@endif ${content}

items.name: hover-card
items.description: A hover/focus-triggered preview card, CSS-only (Radix preview model: no ARIA role, aria-hidden, not focus-trapping). Content lives in the DOM, revealed by group-hover/group-focus-within. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui hover-card partial -- a hover/focus-triggered preview card, CSS-only (no JS). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island used Floating UI + open/close timers + Lit state. Per ADR-0012 the server-pure path is CSS: the panel content is ALREADY in the DOM and is revealed by the group's :hover / :focus-within, with no client state and no positioning JS. The open/close delay nuance is dropped deliberately (a delay needs a timer = client state); the gap between trigger and panel is kept crossable by laying the panel directly under the trigger with no margin and a small padding bridge. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): Radix's hover-card MODEL (preview affordance, not an announced popup) rendered with a CSS group-hover reveal. Tailwind's group + group-hover / group-focus-within utilities express "show the panel when the trigger area is hovered or focused" without any script -- the canonical CSS-only disclosure. No CSS Anchor Positioning is needed because a preview card sits in normal flow under its trigger (absolute, top-full). A11y (Radix preview model): the trigger is a normal interactive element; the panel carries NO ARIA role and is aria-hidden="true" -- it is a sighted-pointer/keyboard preview, so the same information MUST also be reachable in the page flow for assistive tech. Not focus-trapping. Params: gg.jte.Content trigger the trigger element (a link / button / text). gg.jte.Content content the preview body, revealed on hover/focus. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.lievit.hover-card( trigger = @@`@@ada`, content = @@`

items.description: Inline Lucide <svg> by name, sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens. Zero JS, CSP-clean. The root PARTIAL: every partial/wire template that needs a glyph composes it via @template.lievit.icon(name=...).

files.content:
@import static io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui icon partial -- renders an inline Lucide <svg> by name.

  DELIVERY DECISION (scientific, issue #428): inline-per-name, NOT an SVG sprite.
  Compared against MUI (font/SVG components), Radix (per-icon SVG components), shadcn
  (lucide-react, per-icon, tree-shaken) and Web Awesome (<wa-icon>, font/registry):
    (1) tree-shakeability -- inline-per-name wins: only the icons vendored into
        registry/icons/ exist in the body map, so an adopter ships exactly what they use.
        A sprite ships the whole <symbol> sheet (or needs a build to assemble a per-page
        subset) and an extra request / external <use href> with its CSP+cross-doc quirks.
    (2) ergonomics for a JTE adopter -- "add an icon" = drop a .svg in registry/icons/ and
        re-run generate-icon-map.mjs. No sheet to maintain, no <use> id registry.
    (3) robustness -- no <use href>, no external ref, CSP-clean, zero JS.
  The sprite's only edge (repeated-icon byte savings) is marginal in a gestionale and costs
  the copy-in simplicity, so inline-per-name is objectively best here.

  The uniform <svg> wrapper lives HERE once (sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens); the per-icon
  inner body (paths/circles/...) comes from io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body(name) -- a lievit-OWNED
  facade over an IconResolver SPI, defaulting to the bundled, tree-shaken Lucide set
  (LucideIconResolver). The partial names NO adopter class, so it renders standalone out of the box.
  To ship a different icon set, implement io.lievit.ui.IconResolver and call
  LievitIcons.setResolver(...) once at startup; this call site never changes. The Lucide SVGs
  stroke with currentColor, so colour follows the surrounding text colour or any Tailwind `text-*`
  utility / --lv-color-* override on a wrapping element.

  Usage:
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "trash-2", cssClass = "text-[var(--lv-color-danger)]")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "search", size = "1.25rem")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "settings", label = "Open settings")   // labelled (img role)

  By default the icon is decorative: aria-hidden="true" + focusable="false". Pass a `label`
  to expose it to assistive tech (role="img" + aria-label); then it is NOT aria-hidden.

  Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg (any Lucide SVG), run
  `node registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs`, commit the regenerated map.
--%>
@param String name
@param String size = "var(--lv-icon-size)"
@param String cssClass = ""
@param String label = null
<svg
  xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  viewBox="0 0 24 24"
  width="${size}"
  height="${size}"
  fill="none"
  stroke="currentColor"
  stroke-width="var(--lv-icon-stroke)"
  stroke-linecap="round"
  stroke-linejoin="round"
  class="lv-icon ${cssClass}"
  role="${label == null ? null : "img"}"
  aria-hidden="${label == null ? "true" : null}"
  aria-label="${label}"
  focusable="false"
  data-lucide="${name}"
>$unsafe{body(name)}</svg>

items.name: input-otp
items.description: A segmented one-time-code input rendered server-side: N native single-character <input> slots + a hidden `name` mirror that submits the joined value in a plain POST form (zero wire round-trips mid-entry). The auto-advance / paste-fill / backspace-nav choreography is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer. Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-input-otp> Lit island. WAI one-time-password-field ARIA (role=group + per-slot Character N of M labels).

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-otp partial -- a segmented one-time-code input rendered SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012). N native single-character s + a hidden `name` mirror; the small auto-advance / paste / backspace-nav behaviour is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (input-otp.enhancer.ts), NOT inline script. Pairs with registry/jte/input-otp.enhancer.ts. TIER: PARTIAL (+ enhancer). The OTP value is NOT server state mid-entry: there is nothing for the server to decide between keystrokes, so round-tripping each keystroke over the wire would be pure chat for no gain. The slots are server-rendered native inputs; the value is form-submitted (the server binds it at POST via the hidden mirror under `name`), so it is server-bound at the moment that matters. The only client bit is the focus choreography (auto-advance, paste-fill, backspace step-back), which is a tiny typed-TS module the strict CSP accepts (no inline on* handlers). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI one-time-password-field (Radix), the model the dropped Lit island cited: a role="group" of real slots, each aria-label="Character N of M", the per-type inputmode, autocomplete="one-time-code" on the first slot so the platform SMS-autofill fills the whole code, and a visually-hidden mirror so `new FormData(form)` sees the joined value with zero JS. Native inputs beat a custom widget: the platform owns focus, IME, paste and autofill; the enhancer only nudges focus between slots. SEPARATOR + GROUPS: `groupSize` splits the slots into explicit visual groups (shadcn's InputOTPGroup), with an InputOTPSeparator (a Lucide dash) rendered between groups. `groupSize=3` on a length-6 code gives `***-***`. The separator is decorative (aria-hidden); the slots stay one continuous keyboard + value sequence (the enhancer indexes by data-otp-slot, not by group). onComplete: when set, the enhancer fires a `lievit:otp-complete` CustomEvent (detail = the joined value) on the root once every slot is filled, and -- if `onComplete="submit"` -- submits the enclosing
. Wire it to a wire action via the data-otp-complete attribute (the consuming wire template reads it), or listen for the event. Server-first: still no per-keystroke round-trip. A11y (WAI one-time-password-field): div[role=group][aria-label] wrapping N inputs each aria-label="Character N of M"; aria-invalid mirrored on the group + slots. Separators are aria-hidden decorations between visual groups. Params: name String submit field name; the joined value POSTs under it via the hidden mirror length int number of code slots (default 6) value String prefilled value (default empty); chars distributed across the slots type String "numeric" | "alphanumeric" | "alpha" (default "numeric"); drives inputmode + pattern mask boolean render slots as password fields (default false) disabled boolean disable every slot (default false) invalid boolean error ring + aria-invalid (default false) groupSize int split slots into groups of this size with a separator between (0 = no grouping) onComplete String "submit" submits the form when filled; any non-blank value enables the complete event label String accessible label for the slot group (default "One-time code") Usage: @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, type = "numeric") @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, groupSize = 3, onComplete = "submit") import the enhancer once from your entry module: import "./lievit/input-otp.enhancer"; --%> @param String name @param int length = 6 @param String value = "" @param String type = "numeric" @param boolean mask = false @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean invalid = false @param int groupSize = 0 @param String onComplete = null @param String label = "One-time code" !{var inputMode = type.equals("numeric") ? "numeric" : "text";} !{var pattern = type.equals("numeric") ? "[0-9]*" : (type.equals("alpha") ? "[a-zA-Z]*" : "[a-zA-Z0-9]*");} !{var slotType = mask ? "password" : "text";} !{var grouped = groupSize > 0;} !{var hasComplete = onComplete != null && !onComplete.isBlank();}
@for(int i = 0; i < length; i++) @if(grouped && i > 0 && i % groupSize == 0) @template.lievit.icon(name = "minus", size = "1rem") @endif !{var ch = i < value.length() ? String.valueOf(value.charAt(i)) : "";} !{var runStart = i == 0 || (grouped && i % groupSize == 0);} !{var runEnd = i == length - 1 || (grouped && (i + 1) % groupSize == 0);} !{var borderColor = invalid ? "var(--lv-color-danger)" : "var(--lv-color-border)";} !{var leftBorder = runStart ? ("1px solid " + borderColor) : "none";} !{var radius = runStart && runEnd ? "var(--lv-radius-sm)" : (runStart ? "var(--lv-radius-sm) 0 0 var(--lv-radius-sm)" : (runEnd ? "0 var(--lv-radius-sm) var(--lv-radius-sm) 0" : "0"));} @endfor

items.name: navigation-menu
items.description: A site navigation landmark (<nav>, not a menu role) with real <a href> links and optional rich dropdown panels revealed on hover/focus-within, CSS-only (Radix NavigationMenu model). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 2): active-state comes from the server-rendered route via aria-current, the panel reveal is pure CSS, so there is no client state and no wire component. Replaces the <lv-navigation-menu> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui navigation-menu partial -- a site navigation landmark with real links and optional rich dropdown panels, CSS-only (no JS, no client state). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 2): the Lit island is gone. TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL, not WIRE): a navigation menu is, by the ADR's driving rule, "pure display, no client state" once you accept the server-pure overlay. The links are real (the browser navigates, middle-click + open-in-new-tab work); which entry is current is a fact the SERVER already knows (the route being rendered), passed in as `activeKey` -- not client state. The only interactive bit, "reveal the panel", needs no server round-trip and no durable state, so it is pure CSS :hover / :focus-within (the same server-pure disclosure the hover-card partial uses). There is therefore nothing to hold server-side: no @Wire field, no action, no wire component. A WIRE conversion would round-trip the server just to flip a CSS-revealable panel -- strictly worse. Hence `registry:jte`, the simplest thing that works server-first. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): NOT a menu (no role="menu"); a navigation landmark, Radix NavigationMenu as the model the dropped island cited:
wrapping a
    of items. An item is either a bare link (navigation-menu/link) or a trigger that reveals a panel of real links (navigation-menu/menu). The panel reveal is CSS group-hover / group-focus-within (Tailwind `group` + `group-hover` / `group-focus-within`), the canonical CSS-only disclosure -- no @floating-ui/dom, no Anchor Positioning needed (the panel sits in normal flow under its trigger). The intent-delay open / leave-close nuance of the island is dropped deliberately (a delay needs a timer = client state); a small padding bridge keeps the trigger->panel gap crossable. A11y (Radix NavigationMenu model):
    landmark; the panel triggers are

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui navigation-menu/link partial -- one real entry of a navigation-menu, either a top-level bare link or a link inside a rich panel (Wave 2, server-first, no JS). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A plain anchor; navigation is the browser's. The active link carries aria-current="page" + a token-styled highlight, derived server-side from the `active` param (which route is being rendered), never client state. items.name: pagination
items.description: Page navigation: previous / next plus a windowed list of numbered page links with ellipsis gaps. Real <a href> per page, so it works JS-off; the caller passes the page math (current + total) and the partial derives the visible window (MUI-style) with shadcn's markup/token styling. A labelled <nav> landmark, aria-current on the active page, aria-disabled boundary controls.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: pagination (static JTE partial) Page navigation: previous / next plus a windowed list of numbered page links with ellipsis gaps. Mirrors shadcn's Pagination / PaginationContent / PaginationItem / PaginationLink / PaginationPrevious / PaginationNext / PaginationEllipsis. Tier rationale (planning two-tier model): pure presentation, no client state. The page links are REAL elements (server-rendered hrefs), so the control works with JavaScript disabled, which a Lit island could not claim. JTE partial it is. SHAPE DECISION (scientific rule). shadcn/MUI/Radix were compared for how the page list is produced. shadcn leaves the item list to the caller (hand-written s); MUI's DERIVES the windowed list (first, last, current +/- siblings, ellipsis for gaps) from count/page/siblingCount. MUI wins criterion (4) ergonomics + (5) robustness for the no-JS server case: the caller passes the page math (current + total), the partial computes the visible window once, so every page renders a correct, consistent control with no per-call boilerplate. We adopt MUI's derivation with shadcn's markup/token styling. HREF: page links are built from a printf-style `hrefPattern` (one %d placeholder for the page number), e.g. "/contatti?page=%d". This keeps the partial query-string-agnostic: the caller owns the URL shape, the partial only substitutes the number. A real per page. Accessibility (shadcn + APG): the root is
so AT lands on a named landmark; the active page carries aria-current="page" and is a non-link (you cannot navigate to where you already are); prev/next at the boundary become aria-disabled (still in the DOM for layout, not focusable); the ellipsis is aria-hidden with an sr-only "More pages". The chevron icons are decorative (Lucide partial, aria-hidden) with sr-only text labels. Params: int current the active page (1-based). int total the total number of pages (>= 1). String hrefPattern printf pattern for a page URL; one %d = the page number. int siblings pages shown either side of current before collapsing to "...". Default 1. String ariaLabel the
accessible name. Default "Pagination". String prevLabel sr-only text for the previous control. Default "Previous page". String nextLabel sr-only text for the next control. Default "Next page". String klass extra classes on the
. asChild / router-Link (server-first): shadcn's PaginationLink takes `asChild` so the page links can be a framework router-Link instead of a bare . Server-side the element stays a real (no-JS correctness), but the caller can OPT IN extra attributes on every navigable link via `linkAttrs` (a raw attribute string, output with $unsafe so it lands as markup, not escaped text), e.g. an htmx/Turbo/router hook that upgrades the anchor to an in-place navigation: `linkAttrs = "hx-boost=\"true\" hx-target=\"#list\""`. This is the server-first analogue of asChild: same accessible anchor, with the adopter's router wiring attached. Left blank, nothing is injected. Only the navigable links (prev / numbered / next) receive it; the disabled boundary spans and the ellipsis do not. Usage: @@template.lievit.pagination(current = 3, total = 10, hrefPattern = "/contatti?page=%d") @@template.lievit.pagination(current = page, total = totalPages, hrefPattern = "/listings?p=%d&q=" + query, siblings = 2) @@template.lievit.pagination(current = page, total = totalPages, hrefPattern = "/listings?p=%d", linkAttrs = "hx-boost=\"true\" hx-target=\"#list\"") --%> @param int current @param int total @param String hrefPattern @param int siblings = 1 @param String ariaLabel = "Pagination" @param String prevLabel = "Previous page" @param String nextLabel = "Next page" @param String linkAttrs = "" @param String klass = "" !{var page = Math.max(1, Math.min(current, total));} !{var hasPrev = page > 1;} !{var hasNext = page < total;} <%-- Windowed page list (MUI derivation): first + last always, current +/- siblings, gaps -> -1 sentinel (ellipsis). --%> !{var pages = new java.util.ArrayList();} !{var lastShown = 0;} @for(int p = 1; p <= total; p++) !{boolean show = p == 1 || p == total || (p >= page - siblings && p <= page + siblings);} @if(show) @if(lastShown != 0 && p - lastShown > 1) !{pages.add(-1);} @endif !{pages.add(p);} !{lastShown = p;} @endif @endfor
    <%-- Previous --%>
  • @if(hasPrev) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-left", size = "1rem") Prev @else @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-left", size = "1rem") Prev @endif
    • <%-- Numbered pages + ellipsis --%> @for(int p : pages)
  • @if(p == -1) @template.lievit.icon(name = "ellipsis", size = "1rem") More pages @elseif(p == page) ${p} @else ${p} @endif
    • @endfor <%-- Next --%>
  • @if(hasNext) Next @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-right", size = "1rem") @else Next @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-right", size = "1rem") @endif

items.name: popover
items.description: A click-triggered floating panel anchored to a trigger, with ZERO JS. The load-bearing overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): show/hide via the native HTML `popover` attribute + `popovertarget` (top-layer, light-dismiss, ARIA wired by the browser); position via CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area + flip-block fallback), no @floating-ui/dom. Panel content is OWNED, server-rendered markup (a Content slot), never a <slot>. Replaces the <lv-popover> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean. For server-data-driven content + server-held open-state, see the popover-wire component.

TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island held `open` in Lit reactive state, projected the trigger + body through native s (inert in light DOM, so the body silently never rendered -- the bug ADR-0012 was written to kill) and positioned with @floating-ui/dom. Per ADR-0012 R4 the server-pure path replaces ALL of that with platform primitives: - SHOW/HIDE = the native HTML `popover` attribute. A

Pass a unique `id` (the popovertarget link target), the `trigger` content (label/icon) and the `content` panel body (server-rendered, never a <slot>); set `placement` ("bottom" | "top", flips when cramped) or `disabled`. The browser owns show/hide + Escape/outside-click dismiss + aria-expanded; CSS anchor positioning places the panel. The source is yours: edit freely. For a popover whose content depends on server state (the calendar-filter feed), use the popover-wire component instead.

items.name: popover-wire
items.description: The server-data-driven variant of the popover overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): open-state lives in typed Java and the panel CONTENT is (re)derived from server state on each open (gest's calendar-filter feed). The server re-renders the conditionally-rendered panel, the client morphs it; no <slot>, no @floating-ui/dom, no Lit. Positioning stays the server-pure CSS Anchor Positioning the popover PARTIAL uses. Use the popover JTE partial for a static popover; this heavier WIRE tier is for server-held content + open-state.

items.name: progress
items.description: A determinate or indeterminate progress bar with WAI-ARIA progressbar semantics; respects prefers-reduced-motion. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui progress partial -- a determinate or indeterminate progress bar. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island computed a width and an ARIA value; both are pure functions of the typed `value`, so the server renders them. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a token-styled bar with the WAI-ARIA progressbar pattern rather than the native element. The native element's fill cannot be themed by the --lv-* tokens cross-browser (its pseudo-elements vary by engine), and an indeterminate native bar gives no styling hook for the reduced-motion guard; a div+role progressbar (shadcn's Radix Progress approach) themes cleanly and keeps the indeterminate animation in CSS (motion-reduce:animate-none). The track + fill read --lv-color tokens: the track is the primary tinted at 20% (shadcn bg-primary/20) and both are fully rounded (shadcn rounded-full); the fill carries data-slot="progress-indicator" (shadcn's slot name). A11y (WAI-ARIA progressbar): role="progressbar" + aria-valuemin/max + aria-label; for a determinate bar aria-valuenow carries the percent, for indeterminate (value < 0) it is omitted (the indeterminate convention) and the fill animates. Params: int value = -1 0..100 determinate; < 0 => indeterminate. String label = "Progress" accessible name. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the track. Usage: @@template.lievit.progress(value = 42, label = "Uploading") @@template.lievit.progress() (no value -> indeterminate) --%> @param int value = -1 @param String label = "Progress" @param String cssClass = "" !{var indeterminate = value < 0;} !{var pct = indeterminate ? 0 : Math.min(100, Math.max(0, value));}
@if(indeterminate)
@else
@endif

items.name: rich-select
items.description: A searchable Combobox (Filament Select parity, roadmap L1) whose options are filtered SERVER-SIDE: the query binds l:model.debounce, the server re-filters the catalog and re-renders the listbox (the canonical wire/htmx typeahead, no client filtering). Supports single-select (the `selected` string, set via $set) AND multiple-select with removable chips (the `selectedValues` list, toggled via $set('toggleValue',...)), preload (eager full-catalog on mount for small sets) vs lazy debounced search (large sets), inline create-option (a no-match query arms a new value), and rich option labels (avatar/icon + subtext). A tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer adds APG keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End/Enter). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-rich-select> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG combobox + listbox.
items.docs: Register RichSelectComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `allOptions` from your repository in the constructor or a @LievitRender hook (the seed list is placeholder data). The query filters server-side via l:model.debounce. Single mode: selection sets the `selected` field via $set('selected', '<value>'). Multiple mode (multiple=true): each pick / chip-remove toggles membership of its value in `selectedValues` via $set('toggleValue', '<value>'); the chosen options render as removable chips and submit as repeated hidden inputs (name=...). Preload (preload=true) shows the full catalog on mount for SMALL sets; leave it false and narrow `allOptions` server-side per keystroke for LARGE sets. allowCreate=true offers a 'Create "<query>"' affordance on a no-match query (value creation only; wire createOptionUsing server-side for a full form). Rich labels: an Option carries optional avatar (image URL) / icon (Lucide name) + subtext. Call enhanceRichSelects(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() for APG keyboard navigation. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/rich-select")` name assumes the .jte landed under lievit/; adjust freely once copied (the source is yours).

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui separator partial -- a thin divider line between content groups. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A separator is a single styled rule with no client state; the former island held nothing the server cannot render. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG separator pattern. shadcn/Radix render a
with aria-orientation; the platform has no native horizontal-rule element that also carries orientation, so the role-on-div approach wins (it expresses both orientations and stays decoratable). A purely decorative divide passes decorative=true, which drops it from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden) per the APG note that a separator with no semantic meaning should not be announced. A11y (WAI-ARIA separator): role="separator" + aria-orientation when announced; when decorative, role and aria-orientation are dropped and aria-hidden="true" hides the rule. shadcn/Radix default decorative=true (a divider carries no semantic meaning by default), so this defaults to true; pass decorative=false to announce a meaningful divide. Params: String orientation = "horizontal" "horizontal" | "vertical". boolean decorative = true true => hidden from assistive tech (no role); shadcn default. items.name: sheet
items.description: A modal side panel that slides in from any of the four viewport edges (the richer, shadcn-shaped generalisation of drawer): structured header/description/footer + a built-in Lucide close button. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the body + footer are owned template regions, not slots. Replaces the <lv-sheet> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.
