@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sheet WIRE template -- a modal side panel that slides in from any of the four viewport edges (the richer, shadcn-shaped generalisation of drawer) whose open state lives server-side in SheetComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/sheet/SheetComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires `open` and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the header / body / footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children". STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the title id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Sheet"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - the `side` field positions the panel against one edge (right/left/top/bottom); - a built-in close button (showClose) renders the vendored Lucide `x` via @template.icon; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-sheet hooks). Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the title + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) side String edge to anchor: "right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom" heading String panel title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty omits the title) description String panel description; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) showClose boolean render the top-right close button dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

@if(dismissible) @endif
@if(showClose) @endif

items.docs: Register SheetComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.sheet(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it; set `side` to 'right' (default) | 'left' | 'top' | 'bottom', `heading` + `description` for the labelled header, `showClose=false` to hide the close button. Put your sheet body + footer markup directly in the data-lv-sheet-body / data-lv-sheet-footer regions of the copied template (owned source, NOT slots). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-sheet-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). For the minimal heading+body edge panel use the drawer wire instead. Adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: sidebar
items.description: The application sidebar rendered server-side: a <nav> landmark of real <a href> nav items (groups -> menu-items -> collapsible sub-items via native <details>), aria-current on the active entry, plus a collapse/expand rail and a mobile off-canvas overlay. Server-first PARTIAL + a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (ADR-0012, Wave 3); replaces the <lv-sidebar> Lit island. The nav is server-rendered (which item is current is a server fact); the collapse is pure client cosmetic with localStorage persistence, never a wire round-trip. shadcn Sidebar anatomy (header/content/footer, group/menu/menu-sub), WAI-ARIA navigation landmark.

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui sidebar partial -- the application sidebar: a
landmark of real nav items (composed from sidebar/group + sidebar/item) PLUS a collapse/expand control. Server-first PARTIAL + a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (sidebar.enhancer.ts), NOT a Lit island (ADR-0012, Wave 3). Pairs with registry/jte/sidebar/{group,item}.jte + registry/jte/sidebar.enhancer.ts. TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL + enhancer, NOT WIRE): the blueprint classes sidebar as WIRE(PARTIAL): "nav is PARTIAL, collapse is tiny vanilla/wire state". Both halves resolve to server-pure: (1) NAV: real entries. Which one is current is a fact the SERVER already knows (the route being rendered), passed in as the item's `active` -> aria-current="page" -- not client state. Groups / menu-items / sub-items come in via @param Content composed from the sub-partials, filled by the caller from its typed model (gest's shell.jte already iterates a typed NavTab list into s the same way). No data is hardcoded in the partial. (2) COLLAPSE: a rail-width toggle + mobile off-canvas open/close. This is PURE CLIENT COSMETIC: there is nothing for the server to decide when a user narrows their own sidebar, so round-tripping it over the wire would be chat for no gain (the same reasoning input-otp's enhancer uses). Per the blueprint's own fallback -- "pure client cosmetic -> a registry:jte partial + a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS toggle (localStorage persistence), NO Lit" -- the collapse is the enhancer, and the choice persists to localStorage. items.name: slider
items.description: A token-styled native <input type=range>. Carries the slider role, aria-valuemin/max/now and keyboard navigation for free; POSTs the numeric value, binds via l:model. Server-first replacement for the Lit island.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui slider partial -- a token-styled native ; single-thumb by default, an optional two-thumb RANGE variant, horizontal or vertical. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The native range input carries role=slider, aria-valuemin/max/now and full keyboard navigation (Arrow/Home/End/PageUp-Down) for free; there is no client state and no JS key handler (the Lit island wrapped exactly this native element). It POSTs its numeric value under `name`; `l:model` binds the native input/change events. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's Slider is a Radix multi-thumb component (JS for the thumb math) that breaks JS-off submit. For a single-thumb control the native gives the slider role, value semantics and keyboard for free and POSTs natively, so it wins on a11y, bundle and no-JS. The RANGE (two-thumb) case is shadcn's Slider with two values: we render TWO native ranges, one per bound (POSTing `Min` / `Max`), overlaid on a shared track so both thumbs are real native sliders with real keyboard + a11y, no JS thumb math. VERTICAL uses the platform's native vertical range (writing-mode: vertical), not a rotate hack, so the keyboard arrows map to the visual axis. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG slider): each thumb is a real with its own id/name; min/ max/step and the value semantics come from the platform. `ariaLabel` (single, or the lower thumb in range) or `ariaLabelMax` (the upper thumb in range) name the thumbs when there is no visible

items.name: spinner
items.description: A busy indicator with role=status and an accessible label; respects prefers-reduced-motion. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui spinner partial -- a busy indicator (CSS-animated ring). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island only spun a bordered ring with CSS; the animation is pure CSS and carries no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a CSS border-spinner (shadcn/MUI both ship the same rotating-ring primitive). The rotation is a single @keyframes on a Tailwind animate utility; no SVG, no JS. The reduced-motion guard is expressed with motion-reduce:animate-none so the whole thing stays in the markup layer. A11y (WAI-ARIA status): role="status" + an accessible label via aria-label, so assistive tech announces the busy state without seeing the animation. Decorative-only ring uses aria-hidden via the surrounding text. Params: String size = "sm" "sm" (16px, shadcn default) | "md" (24px) -- ring diameter via tokens. String label = "Loading" accessible name announced by assistive tech. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the spinner. Usage: @@template.lievit.spinner() @@template.lievit.spinner(size = "md", label = "Saving") --%> @param String size = "sm" @param String label = "Loading" @param String cssClass = "" !{var sizeClass = "md".equals(size) ? "size-[1.5rem]" : "size-[1rem]";}

We accept the role-switch-vs-role-checkbox trade-off in favour of native semantics + no JS; a checkbox reads as a binary toggle and both patterns are valid for an on/off control (WAI-ARIA). items.name: tabs
items.description: A tab bar + panels where the active tab is server state; only the active panel is shown. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the active id lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-tabs> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG tabs (tablist > tab[aria-selected][aria-controls] + tabpanel[aria-labelledby], roving tabindex). An HTMX content-swap recipe is the lighter alternative for independent panel fragments (see docs).
items.docs: Register TabsComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `tabIds` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `disabledIds`, as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.tabs(...)`. Each tab switches with `l:click="$set('active','<id>')"` (the active tab is a scalar, so the $set magic IS the select; no custom action needed); the @LievitRender hook falls back to the first enabled tab and refuses a disabled/unknown active id. The panel BODY per tab is OWNED markup inside the `data-tabs-panel` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). HTMX alternative: drop the wire and give each tab link an `hx-get` to a controller endpoint returning the panel partial, swapped into the panel container, when the panels are independent server fragments. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/tabs")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.description: A transient overlay notification rendered server-side from a flash / notification: severity drives both colour and the ARIA live role (danger/warning are assertive role=alert, info/success are polite role=status) plus a leading Lucide icon. Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-toast> Lit island.

items.name: toggle-group
items.description: A set of toggle buttons with single- or multiple-selection; the selected value(s) are server state. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the selection lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-toggle-group> Lit island. Radix ToggleGroup a11y: single = radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple = group > button[aria-pressed].
items.docs: Register ToggleGroupComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `values` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `icons` (index-aligned, '' = none) and `disabledValues`, plus `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') and the whole-group `disabled`, as locked server config; the buttons render themselves. Each item toggles with `l:click="$set('toggleValue','<value>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the selected set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side; single mode keeps at most one and allows deselect-to-empty, Radix behaviour). Single mode renders role=radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple renders role=group > button[aria-pressed]. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/toggle-group")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: tooltip
items.description: A floating contextual label, CSS-only; WAI-ARIA tooltip pattern (panel role=tooltip + the trigger's aria-describedby). Revealed by group-hover/group-focus-within. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui tooltip partial -- a floating contextual label, CSS-only (no JS). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island used Floating UI + Lit state to toggle a panel. Per ADR-0012 the server-pure path is CSS: the label text is already in the DOM and revealed by the wrapper's :hover / :focus-within. No positioning JS; the label sits above the trigger in flow (absolute, bottom-full). Escape-to-dismiss (an island nicety needing a listener) is dropped; leaving / blurring the trigger hides it, which satisfies the APG dismissable requirement for a CSS tooltip. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG tooltip pattern rendered with Tailwind group-hover / group-focus-within. The trigger references the label via aria-describedby (the APG contract) so assistive tech announces the description on focus; the visual reveal is the CSS group toggle. A real tooltip role + aria-describedby is what makes this a tooltip rather than a decorative title attribute, so both are kept. A11y (WAI-ARIA tooltip): panel has role="tooltip" + a stable id; the trigger carries aria-describedby pointing at that id, so the label is announced on hover AND on keyboard focus. Params: gg.jte.Content trigger the element the tooltip describes. String content the tooltip label text. String tipId = "lv-tooltip" id linking aria-describedby to the label. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.lievit.tooltip(content = "Save document", tipId = "save-tip", trigger = @@` `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content trigger @param String content @param String tipId = "lv-tooltip" @param String cssClass = "" ${trigger} ${content}
